item.title: People who ban books should be penciled in for a sudden visit from Pennywise the Clown.
item.dc:creator: Lassannn

item.title: Rich kids can do art for a living
item.description: John Adams, second president of the United States, understood this as well: "I must study politics and war that my sons may have liberty to study mathematics and philosophy. My sons ought to study mathematics and philosophy, geography, natural history, naval architecture, navigation, commerce, and agriculture, in order to give their children a right to study painting, poetry, music, architecture, statuary, tapestry, and porcelain."
item.dc:creator: ChemistryAndPhysicsAreFun

item.title: We should talk about Bruno. And Otto.
item.description:
As again Holocaust Memorial Day comes and goes, I present to you two histories of people like you and I, and the choices they made as the Prisoners #1 and #2 of Auschwitz Concentration Camp. THE FIRST: Bruno Brodniewicz was born in 1895 in Poznan, then part of Germany. He was classified as a habitual criminal upon arrest in 1934 by the new Nazi regime and imprisoned in Lichtenburg concentration camp (at this point, still very much a traditional 'prison', housed inside a castle). He was later transferred to Sachsenhausen concentration camp sometime between 1934-1940. On 20 May 1942, he and 29 other prisoners from Sachsenhausen were taken by SS junior office Gerhard Palitzsch to the newly-opened Auschwitz concentration camp. Brodniewicz and the other 29 "green" criminals (so-called as they wore green triangles, indicating they were criminals rather than political, religious, or ethnic prisoners) were selected by Palitzsch to take leadership positions over incoming groups of new prisoners as what became known as "Kapos", prisoners invested with small luxuries (such as private rooms) and extensive authority over others. Gerhard Palitzsch (27 years old in 1942) was a man of exceptional sadism even amongst the SS, so it seems likely that he had deliberate intent in selecting Brodniewicz and other criminals for this role. Palitzsch is believed responsible for over 25,000 individual "back of the head" gunshot executions at the Auschwitz 'Death Wall'; including the execution of a family of five witnessed by survivor Boleslaw Zbozien (inmate number 22557), in which Palitzsch shot the children from youngest - a baby - to oldest, then the parents. Palitzsch was eventually dismissed from the SS for "racial defilement" - rape of Jewish prisoners - and is believed to have died while serving in a Wehrmacht Penal Unit in the Battle of Budapest in 1944.
[Picture: SS Sergeant Major Gerhard Palitzsch. An ironic victim of the racist ideals he served with utter brutality.] Brodniewicz took to this hideously cynical role - freeing up Nazis from the 'stress' of daily foregoing their humanity and handing the job to selected victims - with relish. He is believed responsible for the murder of an unknown amount of prisoners. As "camp elder" (Lagerältester) his decisions on how he would 'work' the inmates under his control decisively set the atmosphere of inter-prisoner relations in Auschwitz; creating a prisoner culture of exploitation, brutality, and manipulation, with green Kapos like Brodniewicz exploiting ethnic and political divisions amongst prisoners for their own benefit while maintaining good relations with the SS via continuous exemplary brutality on their charges. He was a 'victim' of this atmosphere himself when another green Kapo reported Brodniewicz's small horde of gold and valuables he had shaked down from inmates; Brodniewicz was briefly sent to Block 11 ("the punishment bunker", in which the first use of Zyklon B at Auschwitz was to take place), but subsequently Brodniewicz was circulated amongst a large number of Auschwitz subcamps, each time as "camp elder", in what appears to have been a deliberate effort by the SS to use him as a means of ensuring prisoner solidarity was undermined.
[Picture: the armband of an oberkapo, a work-group overseer, given free rein to beat and kill other prisoners in support of Nazi objectives.] Brodniewicz was, in order, made camp elder in the Romani camp in December 1942, in June 1943 in Jaworzno sub-camp, from April 1944 in sub-camp Eintrachthütte and from September 1944 in sub-camp Bismarckhütte. After Auschwitz was partially evacuated in January 1945 as the Red Army closed in, Brodniewicz was transferred to the Woffleben subcamp of Dora concentration camp. Finally, sometime between January and April 1945, he was moved to Bergen-Belsen concentration camp. On April 15 1945 that camp was liberated by British forces. Brodniewicz was lynched along with 200 other Kapos by the now-free prisoners. Today "Kapo" is an incredibly virulent intra-Jewish slur, particularly in Israel. There are some indications in survivor testimonies that some Kapos attempted in small ways to help their charges, or create group solidarity (such as encouraging exemplary production) for the benefit of prisoner-overseer and overseen as a whole, but this was the exception rather than the rule; with such Kapos often losing their privileged position for being seen as 'soft' by the SS, in what again appears to have been a deliberate Nazi policy of ensuring prisoners turned on one another. One such exception was...
THE SECOND: Otto Küsel (born 16 May 1909), Auschwitz inmate number 2, was a burglar from Berlin. Like Brodniewicz, Otto Küsel was made an overseer, but also served as the administator of the inmate Arbeitsdienst (labour service), so he would decide on the type of work assigned to prisoners. Küsel would assign exhausted prisoners to less demanding teams, and would assign prisoners active in the various attempts to plot resistance against the Nazis into teams which had long periods of relatively private interaction, such as those working the kitchens, hospital, or potato storage units. In the memoirs of a large number of political prisoners, including resistance leader Captain Witold Pilecki, he is unambiguously described as a saviour. Küsel would ensure that prisoners who were prepared to attempt escape were assigned to work groups who went outside the camp perimeter. As an overseer perk Küsel was permitted a violin by the SS, and he taught himself to play the Polish national anthem, which he would play to - and sing along with - prisoners while they worked. It was Küsel who reported Brodniewicz's horde of stolen valuables, and briefly freed Auschwitz from the Kapo's baleful presence.
[Picture: Otto Küsel after the war. In Auschwitz, he "bubbled with good humour and had a gift for putting everyone around him in a good mood." Notably, at one point he took despairing prisoners on to the roof of a barracks block, pointed to the countryside beyond the wire, and told them this would pass, and they'd walk free one day.] Prisoners who testified to his humanity & kindness include Józef Błasiński, Piotr Sadowski, Kazimierz Rawicz, Ryszard Dacko, Artur Krzetuski, Jerzy Pozimski, Stanisław Białas & Erwin Olszówka. Several prisoners, such as Eugeniusz Niedojadło, were penpals with Küsel after the war. He escaped from Auschwitz with three Polish prisoners in December 1942. Recaptured in Warsaw, he was returned to Auschwitz in September 1943 as a normal prisoner, and outraged his SS Guards by pretending to be Polish and not being able to speak German, leading to many beatings. He was transferred to Flossenbürg camp in November 1944, where he remained until liberated on 23 April 1945 by American forces.
[Picture: December 29 1942, Küsel and those he escaped with pictured in a Polish resistance safehouse in Broszkowice, only 3 miles from Auschwitz. L-t-R Mieczycslaw Januzewski, Jan Komski, Otto Küsel, Boleslaw Kuczbara, with Mr and Mrs Harat, whose home it was. The second floor of this house had been converted into an apartment by the Harats, who rented it to a SS officer.] He was proclaimed an honourary Polish citizen in 1946, and was later a key witness at the 1965 Frankfurt Auschwitz trials, in which 750 surviving Auschwitz SS personnel were punished for their roles in the camp. In 1981, the exiled Polish Government (descended from the pre-war Government that escaped to Britain, not the post-war Communist government) awarded him the Polish Gold Cross of Merit. Details are limited on his post-war life, but from his survivor penpals it appears he married, had daughters, and had grandchildren. He died in contented comfort in Schwarzhofen, Bavaria in 1984. The stories of Prisoners 1 and 2 of Auschwitz are a stark illustration that while we are seldom masters of our circumstances, we always retain freedom in how we respond to them. As we stand here in the 21st century, we must always remember that. ----------------------------------------------------- More Holocaust history: Children & the Holocaust: https://imgur.com/gallery/juZQGvY America's forgotten victims of the Holocaust: https://imgur.com/gallery/jVmVfvV Roma: https://imgur.com/gallery/Ck9bBbo An artist, during & after: https://imgur.com/gallery/kHvqtSX Orphans after the war: https://imgur.com/gallery/ERfnPEn Imprisoned by Stalin & Hitler: https://imgur.com/gallery/o7nI7UG LGBT people and the Holocaust: https://imgur.com/gallery/xIbrOJW The hunt for gay history destroyed by Nazis: https://imgur.com/gallery/Goiunle Mentally ill & disabled people and the Holocaust: https://imgur.com/gallery/fb4IEAk Photographing the victims: https://imgur.com/gallery/YKywOzU Kastner: https://imgur.com/gallery/yxsDfew Sugihara: https://imgur.com/gallery/vdW86Xb Perl: https://imgur.com/gallery/je5JkQd Rumkowski: https://imgur.com/gallery/sG3ywZF Coward: https://imgur.com/gallery/hHVzpIu Gerstein: https://imgur.com/gallery/pNQepwe
item.dc:creator: BodicaRexVII

item.title: Wholesome post to brighten your day 592
item.description:
https://imgur.com/gallery/dlhPf00 Wholesome Post 591 https://imgur.com/gallery/F0iONEA Wholesome Post 590 https://imgur.com/gallery/YduVKmO Wholesome Post 589 https://imgur.com/gallery/FtWkLif Wholesome Post 588 https://imgur.com/gallery/ajvoklC Wholesome Post 587 https://imgur.com/gallery/p7pu0ke Wholesome Post 586 https://imgur.com/gallery/XigKo5W Wholesome Post 585
I include a Gofundme link in all of my posts to help raise money for people in need. I hope we can help https://www.gofundme.com/f/help-for-bellas-family
I made a wholesome support server on discord, whether you need support would like to give it or just want to chat with some lovely people and look at some wholesome things it's a nice place. Here's the link if you'd like to join us. https://discord.gg/Y4U422F
I hope this post brightened your day :) and i hope it is a lovely day for you! If you need to talk I am always available on discord (username HaveAGreatDay# 9735) and happy to listen. You matter, and if you are struggling please reach out for help. I made a wholesome support server on discord, whether you need support would like to give it or just want to chat with some lovely people and look at some wholesome things it's a nice place. Here's the link if you'd like to join us. https://discord.gg/Y4U422F
item.dc:creator: ryanjoseph666

item.title: Canada's Truck Convoy
item.description: They came though my small ontario town last night. Of course people went insane. Pretty sure someone got their chest signed by some of the drivers.
